summary: Matched the small 24-byte function `lbMemory_800154BC` by implementing it as a getter for two `uintptr_t` fields from a static `lbMemory_804318B0` struct, updating its header prototype and caller types accordingly. The PR also marked the scratch entry for this function as OK and added a `--no-ok` filter to the scratch-tracker `list` command so completed scratches can be hidden.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c` implementation for `lbMemory_800154BC(uintptr_t* arg0, uintptr_t* arg1)`.
Introduced a local `struct lbMemory_UnkStruct1` with two leading `uintptr_t` fields and padding through `0x6F0`, plus a static instance named `lbMemory_804318B0`.
`lbMemory_800154BC` now writes `lbMemory_804318B0.x0` and `lbMemory_804318B0.x4` into its two output pointer arguments.
Changed the `lbMemory_800154BC` declaration in `lbmemory.h` from untyped `void*, void*` parameters to `uintptr_t*` parameters.
Changed `gmmain.c` ARAM free range locals from `u32` to `uintptr_t` before passing them to `lbMemory_800154BC`.
Adjusted includes in `gmmain.c`, adding `<stddef.h>` and moving `<baselib/particle.h>` ordering.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` to mark `lbMemory_800154BC = OK:0x800154BC` instead of `100%:0x800154BC`.
Extended `tools/scratch-tracker/src/main.rs` so `list` accepts `--no-ok`/`-n` and filters out scratches whose completion is `Completion::Ok`.
smart_moves: Used `uintptr_t` for ARAM address values and output parameters, matching the address-like semantics better than `u32` or `void*` while preserving integer arithmetic in the caller.
Modeled only the known leading fields of `lbMemory_804318B0` and padded the rest of the struct, avoiding overclaiming unknown layout while still enabling a clean match.
Kept the matched function minimal: it simply copies two static struct fields to caller-provided output pointers, which aligns with the 24-byte size target.
Updated the caller’s local variable types together with the callee prototype, preventing mismatched pointer-type usage from becoming entrenched.
Added scratch-tracker filtering for OK entries, which helps future decomp work focus list output on unresolved scratches.
decomp_lessons: For tiny getter-style functions, matching can hinge on accurately representing the backing global/static object and the exact pointer output types.
`uintptr_t` is a useful convention for values that are addresses but are manipulated as integers, such as ARAM start/end ranges.
When a full struct is not understood, define named known fields plus explicit padding rather than inventing semantic field names.
After matching a scratch, update both code and scratch metadata so tooling reflects the current decomp state.
Small matching PRs may include lightweight tooling improvements when they directly support managing scratch status.
naming_conventions: Unknown library memory symbols retain address-based names such as `lbMemory_800154BC` and `lbMemory_804318B0`.
Unknown struct types can use an explicit placeholder name like `lbMemory_UnkStruct1`.
Partially understood fields use offset-style names such as `x0` and `x4`.
Header declarations include address comments such as `/* 0154BC */ void lbMemory_800154BC(...)`.
The header guard changed to an address-style guard `GALE01_014E24` rather than the prior path-style `MELEE_LB_LBMEMORY_H`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declared the static backing struct in the same C file so the generated accesses can be expressed as direct loads from `lbMemory_804318B0.x0` and `.x4`.
Used `uintptr_t*` output parameters so the compiler emits stores of pointer-width integer fields through the two arguments.
Preserved unknown bytes in the backing object with `char x8[0x6F0 - 0x8]`, which fixes the object size without pretending to know its contents.
Changed caller locals to `uintptr_t` to keep argument types consistent with the matched function signature.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that expand or rename `lbMemory_UnkStruct1` or identify fields of `lbMemory_804318B0`.
Search for additional callers of `lbMemory_800154BC` to verify whether `uintptr_t*` stayed the accepted signature.
Check later scratch-tracker PRs for more filtering or scratch status workflow improvements.
Search for PRs around `lbMemory_8001564C` because it is declared in the same header and was listed as already matched in the report.
